title: rubus × semirudis
intro: Rubus × semirudis, also known as thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family, resulting from the cross between Rubus idaeus (red raspberry) and Rubus parviflorus (black raspberry). The medicinal properties of thimbleberry include its high antioxidant content, anti-inflammatory properties, and potential to reduce blood sugar levels. Thimbleberry offers various health benefits, such as supporting heart health, reducing the risk of certain cancers, and providing relief from digestive issues. Thimbleberry can be prepared as a tea, juice, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to leverage its medicinal properties and health benefits.
